title: Orthopogon crus-galli
parts: Unfortunately, I don't have enough information on Orthopogon crus-galli to provide a specific answer regarding its medicinal parts.
side_effects: Orthopogon crus-galli, also known as the "crush bugwort", is a plant that may cause skin irritation or allergic reactions in some individuals upon contact with its sap. In rare cases, ingesting the plant can lead to gastrointestinal issues. The plant's sap may also cause skin burning or redness if not properly washed off. There is limited information available regarding the long-term effects of exposure to Orthopogon crus-galli. More research is needed to fully understand the potential side effects of this plant.
